name: Anju

notes: The current restaurant-issued dinner, brunch, and happy-hour pages contain 51 retained food/nonalcoholic-beverage presentations that merge into 49 canonical products across nine categories.
All 45 frozen rows were reconciled: 30 map to current products, six are parser artifacts, and nine are stale. Nineteen current products were absent from the frozen app data.
Thirteen modifier rows and nine alcohol-only products were excluded. The Beverages category is ordered last.

Thirty of 49 current products have positive major-allergen signals directly supported by current restaurant-issued ingredient text or, for overlapping Mandu and Eomuk details, the restaurant-linked Toast catalog. Nineteen remain unavailable.
No complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure is published online. Printed gluten-free, vegetarian, and dairy-free menus mentioned by the FAQ were not accessible and were not reconstructed.

The FAQ's blanket no-peanut claim conflicts with an explicit current Toast peanut warning. The global negative claim was rejected; no missing term, gluten-free option, or vendor omission was promoted into a safety assurance.
No may-contain claim was invented. Ingredient-intelligence inference remains visibly separate from official source data.
The frozen generic parser crossed paragraph and price boundaries, creating six standalone artifacts such as 1530-Day Kimchi and attaching neighboring descriptions and allergens to the wrong rows.

The reproducible Anju parser reads section headings and price-delimited product rows, attaches only adjacent descriptions, quarantines modifiers, filters alcohol-only rows, and merges duplicate Mandu/Yache Mandu meal-period presentations.

The frozen /happy-hour-1 configuration now returns HTTP 404 and omitted the current /happy-hour food menu. Source roles were corrected without using Toast takeout-only products to expand the dine-in catalog.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Parsed 26 current dinner food presentations across five sections.
HTTP 200. Parsed 18 retained products, quarantined seven modifier rows, and excluded three alcohol-only products.
HTTP 200. Parsed seven current food presentations and excluded six alcohol-only drink rows.
HTTP 404. The frozen Happy Hour URL is retired; current navigation uses /happy-hour.
HTTP 200. Located dietary-menu availability text and the conflicting global no-peanut statement; no complete online allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure was present.
HTTP 200. Confirmed that the restaurant directly links its Take Out button to the Anju Toast catalog.
The public web-rendered catalog returned HTTP 200 and confirmed the address plus item-level Mandu shellfish, Eomuk fish, and Broccolini peanut disclosures. Direct curl was blocked with HTTP 403.
notes: Used only for overlapping item evidence and contradiction analysis; no takeout-only product was added to the primary catalog.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Targeted official-domain searches for allergen, allergy, gluten-free, dairy-free, and PDF sources found the FAQ and linked Toast catalog but no public printed special menu, full matrix, or cross-contact policy.

findings.summary: The frozen extraction created six standalone products by concatenating prices, names, descriptions, and modifiers, and shifted or truncated current content on ten matched rows.
findings.resolution: Replaced the generic output with a section-aware parser that separates products, descriptions, prices, and modifiers and asserts the exact current source shape.

findings.summary: Nine frozen products are absent from every current food-menu surface, while nineteen current products were missing from the app.
findings.resolution: Removed all nine stale products and added all nineteen omitted current products, producing a 49-product canonical catalog.

findings.summary: Eleven of the 30 current frozen rows had missing or overstated positive allergen signals, including Mandu's omitted shellfish/soy evidence and Palace Ddukbokgi's unsupported wheat/gluten/egg signals.
findings.resolution: Recomputed only directly supported current positive signals, preserved unavailable items, and left inference and cross-contact semantics separate.

findings.summary: The configured /happy-hour-1 URL now returns 404 and the frozen record omitted the current /happy-hour food products.
findings.resolution: Replaced the retired URL with the current restaurant-navigation target and included its seven food presentations.

findings.summary: Anju's FAQ says no dish uses peanuts, while its current restaurant-linked Toast catalog explicitly warns that Broccolini Twigim contains peanuts.
findings.resolution: Rejected the blanket negative statement as a safety assurance and retained only item-level positive evidence.

findings.summary: The FAQ references printed dietary menus, but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure is publicly available online.
findings.resolution: Marked 19 current products unavailable where direct positive evidence was insufficient and invented no negative or may-contain claims.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Anju from its current restaurant-issued menu surfaces, removed parser artifacts and stale rows, restored omitted products, corrected source semantics and allergen signals, and preserved ambiguity rather than guessing.
